question: What does an HttpErrorResponse with status === 0 indicate?
options: The server returned an empty response body
The request was cancelled by the user navigating away
A network-level error — no response was received (offline, DNS failure, CORS block)
The server returned HTTP status 0, which means 'no content'

question: What is the purpose of Angular's ErrorHandler class?
options: It handles HTTP errors from the HttpClient only
It catches all unhandled JavaScript errors (including those outside RxJS) that escape other error handling — the last line of defense
It automatically retries failed HTTP requests
It provides a global try/catch around all component templates

question: What is wrong with this code? `catchError(() => EMPTY)` used on every HTTP call without anything else.
options: EMPTY is not a valid return value from catchError
EMPTY causes the application to crash instead of completing the stream
Errors are silently swallowed — no logging, no user notification, bugs become impossible to diagnose
Nothing — this is the recommended pattern for all HTTP error handling

question: You want to retry an HTTP request up to 3 times if it fails, then show an error if all retries fail. Which RxJS operator handles the retry?
options: catchError(retry(3, err => ...))
retry(3) — placed before catchError in the pipe chain
repeat(3)
switchMap with a retry counter

question: An error interceptor intercepts a 500 response and shows a toast notification. Should it also re-throw the error?
options: No — the interceptor handled it, so the error should not propagate further
Yes — re-throw with throwError(() => error) so service-level catchError handlers can also react to it if needed
It depends on whether the service has its own catchError
Only if the component is subscribed with an error handler
